title: Fruits
description: Learn fruits in Filipino Sign Language

pages.title: Welcome to Fruits
pages.content: In this lesson, you'll learn how to sign fruits in Filipino Sign Language (FSL). You'll learn 10 important signs with step-by-step instructions and practice questions.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Apple'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Apple'. A round fruit with firm, white flesh and skin. 

How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the 'X' handshape (index finger bent). 2. Place the knuckle of the index finger on the cheek. 3. Twist the hand once or twice.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Banana'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Banana'. A long, curved fruit with a yellow peel. 

How to sign:
1. Use the dominant '1' handshape. 2. Pull the index finger down on the non-dominant index finger (simulating peeling a banana).

pages.title: How to Sign 'Orange'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Orange'. A round, juicy citrus fruit. 

How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the 'C' handshape. 2. Squeeze the hand twice in front of the mouth.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Grapes'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Grapes'. A small, round, juicy fruit. 

How to sign:
1. Use the dominant '5' handshape, fingers wiggling. 2. Start at the elbow and move the hand down the non-dominant arm (simulating a cluster).

pages.title: How to Sign 'Strawberry'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Strawberry'. A red, juicy fruit with seeds on the surface. 

How to sign:
1. Place the dominant 'R' shape on the chin. 
2. Slide the finger downward twice.
3. Then use the non-dominant handshape 'I' and touch it by all of the fingers to simulate a fruit.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Watermelon'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Watermelon'. A large, round fruit with a hard green rind and sweet, red, pulpy flesh. 

How to sign:
1. Close your non-dominant hand and flick it by the dominant hand.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Mango'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Mango'. A fleshy, oval, yellowish-red tropical fruit. 

How to sign:
1. Use the dominant 'M' handshape to scoop the flat palm of the non-dominant hand.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Pineapple'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Pineapple'. A large, juicy tropical fruit with spiky leaves. 

How to sign:
1. Use the dominant 'P' handshape and move it up the face in an arc.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Peach'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Peach'. A round fruit with fuzzy skin and a large stone. 

How to sign:
1. Use the dominant 'P' handshape and touch it to the cheeks.
2. Twist the 'P' handshape back and forth.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Cherry'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Cherry'. A small, round, red fruit with a stone. 

How to sign:
1. Sign the 'Red Color' sign by placing the tip of the index finger on the chin. 
2. Slide the finger downward twice.
3. Then use the non-dominant handshape 'I' and touch it by all of the fingers to simulate a fruit in a stem.

pages.content: Test your understanding with this question.
